title: 2026 Waste Management Rate Increase Notice
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 17, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Suzanne Groneman, Environmental Services Manager
Cynthia Esparza, Director
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: 2026 Waste Management Rate Increase Notice
On March 6, 2026, Waste Management notified the City of Reno of rate adjustments in accordance with current residential and commercial agreements. The adjustments will take effect on April 1, 2026.
Adjustments are based on the Consumer Index Fund (CPI) for garbage and trash collection. Increases take effect in accordance with Section 6.2 of the agreements and occur when Waste Management's Return on Revenue (ROR) falls below ten percent (10%) on a three-year rolling average.
The CPI increased by 5.27% from December 2024 to December 2025, resulting in a 5.27% increase to all service rates listed in the attached rate sheets.
Attachment:
• Waste Management Notification Letter and Rate Sheets




Northern Nevada Waste Management
100 Vassar Street
Reno, NV 89502
3/6/2026

Ms. Jackie Bryant
City Manager, City of Reno
P.O. Box 1900
Reno, Nevada 89505


Dear Ms. Bryant,
This letter serves as formal notification of upcoming residential and commercial rate adjustments in accordance with the franchise agreements between the City of Reno and WM. These adjustments will take effect on April 1, 2026.
Based on data published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for Garbage and Trash Collection increased by 5.27% for the period from December 2024 through December 2025.
In addition, following the City Council's vote on February 11, 2026, to increase the franchise fee to 14%, WM has prepared updated calculations reflecting the combined impact of both the CPI adjustment and the revised franchise fee for residential and commercial services.
To calculate the new rates, WM first removed the impact of the current 8% franchise fee from existing rates. The resulting base rate was then increased by the 5.27% CPI amount. Finally, the adjusted base rate was updated to reflect the new 14% franchise fee.
Detailed calculations and updated rate tables are included in the following pages.
Please feel free to contact Kendra Kostelecky with any questions or if you would like to discuss these adjustments further.
Sincerely,
Kendra Kostelecky
Waste Management
CC: Suzanne Groneman
Jorge Ballesteros
Maria Davis
Ali Heinle
Jonathan Crabtree
April Puhaida
Sean Banks

fields.authors: Suzanne Groneman, Environmental Services Manager; Cynthia Esparza, Director
fields.tldr: Waste Management notified the City of Reno on March 6, 2026 of rate adjustments effective April 1, 2026, driven by a 5.27% CPI increase for garbage and trash collection from December 2024 to December 2025. Rates are also affected by the City Council's February 11, 2026 vote to raise the franchise fee from 8% to 14%. New rates were calculated by removing the old franchise fee from current rates, applying the CPI increase to the base rate, and then adding the new 14% franchise fee. The memo transmits the formal notification letter and updated rate sheets from Waste Management.
fields.key_stats: CPI increase: 5.27% (December 2024 to December 2025); Franchise fee increase: 8% to 14% (approved February 11, 2026); Rate trigger: Waste Management's Return on Revenue (ROR) falls below 10% on a three-year rolling average; Sample residential rate change: standard 35-gal solid waste / 64-gal recycle cart from $20.56 to $23.15; Senior rate for same service: $18.38 to $20.70; Low Income/Senior rate: $13.79 to $17.36; Animal-resistant 96-gal automated service: $30.64 to $34.51; Cart Replacement: $126.27 to $142.20
fields.key_decisions: City Council voted on February 11, 2026 to increase the franchise fee from 8% to 14%. Rate adjustments are triggered under Section 6.2 of the residential and commercial franchise agreements when Waste Management's ROR falls below 10% on a three-year rolling average.
fields.action_items: Rates take effect April 1, 2026; parties with questions are directed to contact Kendra Kostelecky at Waste Management.
